# Phantasy Documentation

> Verified docs for the flagship companion runtime

Build an AI companion or VTuber who can run her own site, content, automations, and business.

## Getting Started

- [Getting Started](/docs/getting-started): Phantasy is best understood as a companion/VTuber product, not just an install flow.
- [Quickstart](/docs/getting-started/quickstart): This is the fastest path to a usable Phantasy runtime with the current chat surfaces:
- [Introduction](/docs/getting-started/introduction): Phantasy lets you build an AI companion/VTuber who can run her own site, content, automations, and business.
- [Use Cases](/docs/getting-started/use-cases): Phantasy is broad, so the docs should be explicit about the story it is best at: an AI companion/VTuber with a real operating stack around her.
- [Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation): Phantasy is Bun-first for local development and npm-compatible for published package installs.
- [First Run](/docs/getting-started/first-run): This guide gets you from zero to a running Phantasy companion/VTuber product with the current flagship story intact.
- [Configuration](/docs/getting-started/configuration): Phantasy supports config from files, env, and persisted admin settings.
- [Bootstrapping](/docs/getting-started/bootstrapping): This guide covers initializing your Phantasy workspace and the local files that shape agent behavior.

## Guides

- [Business Agent CMS 10 Minutes](/docs/guides/BUSINESS_AGENT_CMS_10_MINUTES): This is the shortest path to the product story that matters most for launch: an AI companion/VTuber who runs her own site, content, automations, and business from one runtime.
- [Authentication Setup](/docs/guides/AUTHENTICATION_SETUP): This guide covers the supported v1 auth paths and the rate limiting that protects them.
- [Deploy](/docs/guides/DEPLOY): The primary self-hosting path is Docker.
- [Consuming Phantasy](/docs/guides/CONSUMING_PHANTASY): Build an AI companion/VTuber, then layer site, content, automations, and business around her on the same foundation.
- [Live2D Setup](/docs/guides/LIVE2D_SETUP): This guide covers the Live2D runtime used by the Phantasy Agent admin UI.
- [Live2D Prod Ready](/docs/guides/LIVE2D_PROD_READY): This document captures the exact configuration that makes Live2D work reliably in development previews and production. Follow it to avoid regressions.
- [Custom Integration Guide](/docs/guides/CUSTOM_INTEGRATION_GUIDE): Custom Integrations let external products talk to your companion through Phantasy's admin-managed REST surface.
- [Environment Setup](/docs/guides/ENVIRONMENT_SETUP): This guide explains how to properly configure environment variables for Phantasy Agent across different deployment scenarios.
- [Storage Options](/docs/guides/STORAGE_OPTIONS): - File-based KV storage is not production-ready
- [OPEN Source FAQ](/docs/guides/OPEN_SOURCE_FAQ): This FAQ is the public-facing answer set for common questions about Phantasy's open-source model.
- [OpenClaw Migration](/docs/guides/OPENCLAW_MIGRATION): Phantasy is not a byte-for-byte clone of OpenClaw, but the local operating model is intentionally close enough that most teams can migrate by moving workspace files instead of rewriting everything.
- [Migration Notes](/docs/guides/MIGRATION_NOTES): 1. **Package Manager**: Use `bun` for installs, scripts, and lockfile management
- [Coolify Compose](/docs/guides/COOLIFY_COMPOSE): Use this when you want the Agent to bring its own Postgres dependency. Coolify manages the stack from the repo's `docker-compose.yml`.
- [Neon Setup](/docs/guides/NEON_SETUP): 1. Go to [console.neon.tech](https://console.neon.tech)
- [Infisical Setup](/docs/guides/INFISICAL_SETUP): Use Infisical for shared or production deployments. Keep only the Infisical bootstrap variables on the host platform. Store the application secrets inside Infisical.
- [R2 Quick Setup](/docs/guides/R2_QUICK_SETUP): ```json
- [R2 CORS Setup](/docs/guides/R2_CORS_SETUP): This guide explains how to configure CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) for your Cloudflare R2 bucket to work with the Phantasy agent media storage system.
- [Release Policy](/docs/guides/RELEASE_POLICY)
- [Multi Agent Setup](/docs/guides/multi-agent-setup): Phantasy supports many runtime configs, but the embedded admin app is single-runtime per deployment. If you need multiple live agents, run separate Phantasy deployments and manage them from Party-HQ.

## Features

- [Chronicle](/docs/features/chronicle): Chronicle is the Phantasy Agent's built-in journaling and blogging system. It enables agents to autonomously generate, review, publish, and syndicate content — functioning as a personal journal, a public blog, and a long-term memory source.

## Integrations

- [Browser Based Providers](/docs/integrations/browser-based-providers): Phantasy supports running AI models entirely in your browser using WebGPU. These providers require no API keys and run locally on your device.
- [Custom](/docs/integrations/custom): Build custom REST API integrations for member apps, storefronts, mobile clients, or other external surfaces.
- [Discord](/docs/integrations/discord): Complete guide for setting up Discord integration.
- [Integrations Guide](/docs/integrations/integrations-guide): Phantasy has five extension surfaces. They solve different problems and should not be mixed together casually.
- [Live2D](/docs/integrations/live2d): 2D avatar integration using Live2D Cubism models.
- [Party HQ](/docs/integrations/party-hq): Party-HQ is the external control plane for multi-agent ops: approvals, chat, workspace context, and runtime visibility. Phantasy is the first-class integration, but the wire contract is framework-agnostic so OpenClaw-likes, Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Paperclip-style runtimes, and custom agents can register the same way.
- [Twitch](/docs/integrations/twitch): Complete guide for setting up Twitch integration.
- [Twitch Setup Guide](/docs/integrations/twitch-setup-guide): This guide will walk you through setting up the Twitch integration for your Phantasy Agent. No coding required!
- [Twitter](/docs/integrations/twitter): Complete guide for setting up Twitter/X integration.
- [Vrm](/docs/integrations/vrm): 3D avatar integration using VRM (Virtual Reality Model) format.

## Built In

- [Image](/docs/plugins/built-in/image): The image plugin provides AI image generation capabilities.
- [Voice](/docs/plugins/built-in/voice): The voice plugin provides text-to-speech capabilities.

## Optional

- [Blockchain](/docs/plugins/optional/blockchain): Optional plugins for blockchain and Web3 functionality.
- [Cache](/docs/plugins/optional/cache): The cache plugin provides intelligent response caching for improved performance.
- [Erc8004](/docs/plugins/optional/erc8004): Get your AI agent registered on the ERC-8004 "Trustless Agents" standard in 5 minutes.
- [Games](/docs/plugins/optional/games): Optional plugins for games and entertainment.

## Platforms

- [Discord](/docs/plugins/platforms/discord): The Discord plugin enables your agent to operate as a Discord bot.
- [Twitch](/docs/plugins/platforms/twitch): The Twitch plugin enables your agent to operate as a Twitch chat bot.
- [Twitter](/docs/plugins/platforms/twitter): The X plugin enables your agent to interact on Twitter/X.

## Plugins

- [Overview](/docs/plugins/overview): Phantasy uses a code-first plugin framework built around:
- [Developing](/docs/plugins/developing): Phantasy uses a WordPress-inspired plugin architecture, but the runtime now treats plugins and built-in capabilities differently:
- [Developing Plugins](/docs/plugins/developing-plugins): This page has moved to [Developing Plugins](./developing.md).
- [Marketplace](/docs/plugins/marketplace): Welcome to the Phantasy Agent Plugin Marketplace! This guide will help you discover, install, and manage plugins to extend your agent's capabilities.
- [Troubleshooting](/docs/plugins/troubleshooting): Quick fixes for common plugin configuration issues.

## API

- [API](/docs/api): ```
- [Rest API](/docs/api/rest-api): OpenAI-compatible REST API endpoints.
- [WebSocket API](/docs/api/websocket-api): Real-time WebSocket API for live updates.
- [Openai Compatible](/docs/api/openai-compatible): Phantasy Agent exposes an OpenAI-compatible API.
- [Standardization](/docs/api/standardization): All Admin API routes must use standardized response wrappers to ensure consistency across the system. This provides predictable error handling, automatic type safety, and seamless frontend integration.

## Architecture

- [Architecture](/docs/architecture): ```
- [Design Principles](/docs/architecture/design-principles): Phantasy lets you build an AI companion/VTuber who can run her own site, content, automations, and business.
- [Agent Compatibility](/docs/architecture/agent-compatibility): Phantasy intentionally supports the common local-agent surfaces people already use across modern coding-agent stacks:
- [Runtime Packages](/docs/architecture/runtime-packages): Phantasy exposes one default package plus focused runtime surfaces for advanced composition.
- [System Design](/docs/architecture/system-design): Phantasy is organized as one product with three layers:
- [Repo Standards](/docs/architecture/repo-standards): `bun run validate:standards` is the repo-specific guardrail layer.
- [Provider Routing](/docs/architecture/provider-routing): Phantasy uses a simple two-tier routing model:
- [Framework Audit](/docs/architecture/framework-audit): This page summarizes the current state of the codebase after the truth-consolidation sweep.
- [Memory System](/docs/architecture/memory-system): Phantasy keeps markdown as the readable zero-config default, but the flagship deployment path is PostgreSQL with `pgvector`.
- [WebSocket System](/docs/architecture/websocket-system): The existing WebSocket implementation (`src/server/websocket-manager.ts`) is **minimal** and only supports:
- [Heartbeat](/docs/architecture/heartbeat): Phantasy implements a heartbeat system inspired by OpenClaw, enabling periodic agent wake-ups for background tasks, maintenance, and proactive behaviors.
- [Admin UI Design System](/docs/architecture/admin-ui-design-system): This is the shared token and primitive contract for Phantasy's admin UI.
- [Admin UI Style Policy](/docs/architecture/admin-ui-style-policy): This is the style contract for Phantasy's admin UI.
- [Architecture Review](/docs/architecture/architecture-review): This page is archived.
- [Compaction](/docs/architecture/compaction): Every model has a **context window** - the maximum tokens it can process. Long-running conversations accumulate messages and tool results. When the conversation nears or exceeds this limit, Phantasy **compacts** older history to stay within limits.
- [Config Sync Permissions](/docs/architecture/config-sync-permissions): How configuration stays in sync across Admin UI and CLI, how authentication works across surfaces, and how users are identified.
- [Conversation Unification](/docs/architecture/conversation-unification): - **Active conversations**: In-memory `conversationHistory` array
- [Database Shape](/docs/architecture/database-shape): Phantasy's primary database contract is PostgreSQL plus an ordered SQL migration set in `migrations/`.
- [Licensing And Governance](/docs/architecture/licensing-and-governance): This memo documents the current recommendation for licensing, trademarks, contributions, and future commercial surfaces.
- [Multi Agent](/docs/architecture/multi-agent): Party-HQ is the multi-agent control plane. The embedded Phantasy admin app is single-runtime per deployment and should not grow a second orchestration layer of its own.
- [No Code Config](/docs/architecture/no-code-config): The Phantasy Agent framework is designed as a **no-code agent CMS/command center** where all configuration happens through the Admin UI dashboard. Config files are just defaults—the database is the source of truth for runtime configuration.
- [Party HQ Control Plane](/docs/architecture/party-hq-control-plane): Party-HQ should be treated as a separate control-plane app for agents, not as a feature folded into the main Phantasy runtime package.
- [Plugin Repository Strategy](/docs/architecture/plugin-repository-strategy): Phantasy has two different plugin concerns, and they should not be conflated:
- [Prompt Caching](/docs/architecture/prompt-caching): Phantasy implements KV-cache-based prompt caching to dramatically reduce token usage and costs for long-running agents.
- [Retry Policy](/docs/architecture/retry-policy): Phantasy implements retry logic to handle transient failures gracefully.

## Deployment

- [Cloud Services](/docs/deployment/cloud-services): Advanced deployment and operator guides for external services.
- [Coolify GitLab](/docs/deployment/coolify-gitlab): Deployment with Coolify and GitLab CI.
- [Environment](/docs/deployment/environment): Complete environment configuration guide.
- [Phala Tee](/docs/deployment/phala-tee): Use this when validating Phantasy inside a Phala CVM or against the local dstack simulator.
- [Storage](/docs/deployment/storage): Configure database and storage backends.

## Development

- [Branching Cicd](/docs/development/branching-cicd): Git workflow and CI/CD pipeline.
- [Code Style](/docs/development/code-style): Code style guidelines for Phantasy Agent.
- [Contributing](/docs/development/contributing): Use this guide when you are opening a pull request against the open-source repo.
- [Documentation](/docs/development/documentation): Public docs live in `apps/docs/src/docs/content`.
- [Refactoring PLAN](/docs/development/REFACTORING_PLAN): > **Created:** January 2026
- [Testing](/docs/development/testing): Phantasy uses a layered release gate. Run the smallest useful check first, then escalate to the full release gate when the surface is broad.

## Generated

- [Defaults](/docs/generated/defaults): This file is generated from `src/config/constants.ts`.
- [Providers](/docs/generated/providers): This file is generated from the registered runtime provider definitions.
- [Plugins](/docs/generated/plugins): This file is generated from the built-in capability descriptors.
- [Skills](/docs/generated/skills): This file is generated from bundled `skills/*/SKILL.md` files.
- [Themes](/docs/generated/themes): This file is generated from bundled `themes/*/theme.json` manifests.
- [Workflows](/docs/generated/workflows): This file is generated from bundled `workflows/*.workflow.json` files.
- [MCP](/docs/generated/mcp): This file is generated from `src/mcp/server-presets.json`.
- [Presets](/docs/generated/presets): This file is generated from `src/config/presets/index.ts`.
- [Admin Tabs](/docs/generated/admin-tabs): This file is generated from the shared admin tab registry used by the layout, URL resolver, and command palette.
- [CLI Commands](/docs/generated/cli-commands): This file is generated from the CLI entrypoint command dispatch table.
- [API Routes](/docs/generated/api-routes): This file is generated from mounted admin route handlers and explicit Express admin router mounts.
- [Package Exports](/docs/generated/package-exports): This file is generated from `package.json` exports.
- [Party HQ Protocol](/docs/generated/party-hq-protocol): This file is generated from `apps/party-hq/packages/shared/src/protocol.ts`.

## Maintenance

- [Updating](/docs/maintenance/updating): Keep your Phantasy installation up to date with regular updates.
